name: git_diff
description: Generates structured, token-efficient diff output from Git repositories using delta (with git diff fallback). Supports working tree, staged, commit range, branch, and single-file modes. Output includes per-file change types, hunks with @@ headers, and classified lines with old/new line numbers ready for agent reasoning.

primary.note: Rust-native syntax-highlighting diff pager. Provides side-by-side view, colourised unified diff, and themeable output. Passes unrecognised flags to git diff.

fallback.note: POSIX git diff. Reduced fidelity — no side-by-side layout, no syntax highlighting, basic colour only. Raw unified diff output.

mode.description: Diff mode. 'working_tree' shows unstaged changes. 'staged' shows index changes. 'commit_range' diff two commits (source..target). 'branch' diff since merge-base (source...target). 'file' diff a specific path.

path.description: Repository root or path filter. Defaults to current directory. Ignored in 'file' mode when target is provided.

target.description: Target commit, branch, or file path depending on mode. Required by commit_range, branch, and file modes.

source.description: Source commit or branch for commit_range and branch modes.

side_by_side.description: Enable side-by-side layout. Delta only; no effect on git fallback.

color.description: Enable colourised output. Normalisation pipeline strips ANSI codes if needed.

ignore_whitespace.description: Ignore all whitespace when comparing lines. Maps to -w.

ignore_space_change.description: Ignore changes in amount of whitespace. Maps to -b.

stat_only.description: Show only diffstat (file change summary) instead of full hunks.

max_files.description: Maximum number of files to include in output. Enforced in the normalisation pipeline.

max_lines_per_file.description: Maximum lines per file diff to include in output. Enforced in the normalisation pipeline.

total_files.description: Number of files with changes in the diff.

total_additions.description: Total lines added across all files.

total_deletions.description: Total lines deleted across all files.

truncated.description: True if output was capped by max_files or max_lines_per_file.

used_fallback.description: True if git diff fallback was invoked instead of delta.

file.description: File path relative to repository root.

old_file.description: Original file path for renames. Null for non-renames.

old_line_number.description: Line number in the old file. Null for additions.

new_line_number.description: Line number in the new file. Null for deletions.

warnings.description: Non-fatal advisories (e.g., binary files skipped, submodule changes omitted).

used_fallback.description: True if git fallback was attempted and also failed.

args: {% if mode == 'commit_range' and source and target %}{{ source }}..{{ target }}{% elif mode == 'commit_range' and target %}{{ target }}{% endif %}
{% if mode == 'branch' and source and target %}{{ source }}...{{ target }}{% endif %}
{% if mode == 'file' and target %}--{{ target }}{% elif path != '.' %}--{{ path }}{% endif %}
execution.note: Delta passes unrecognised flags (such as --stat and -w) to git diff. The -- separator ensures paths are not interpreted as revision names. For file mode, target is the file path.

pipeline.description: Parse unified diff text into structured per-file hunks. Extract file paths, change types, @@ headers, and classify each line as context/addition/deletion with old/new line numbers.

pipeline.action: Slice diffs array to max_files entries.
pipeline.description: Enforce file-level cap to prevent token saturation in large commits.

pipeline.action: For each file, cap total lines across all hunks to max_lines_per_file. Prefer keeping the beginning of the diff (earlier hunks).
pipeline.description: Enforce per-file line cap. Sets truncated flag if any file is capped.
Roll up per-file metrics into top-level totals for agent reasoning.
Merge all computed fields into the final success envelope. Set mode from inputs.mode. Set used_fallback to false. Set execution_ms from runtime timer.

args: {% if mode == 'commit_range' and source and target %}{{ source }}..{{ target }}{% elif mode == 'commit_range' and target %}{{ target }}{% endif %}
{% if mode == 'branch' and source and target %}{{ source }}...{{ target }}{% endif %}
{% if mode == 'file' and target %}--{{ target }}{% elif path != '.' %}--{{ path }}{% endif %}
output_gaps: side_by_side layout is unavailable (git diff has no side-by-side mode)
syntax highlighting is unavailable; only basic ANSI colour codes are emitted
hunk headers are plain text; delta-enhanced headers (line numbers, blame info) are absent
file rename detection heuristics may differ between git diff and delta
stat_only produces plain text stat blocks instead of delta-rendered tables

compatible_downstream.description: Pass modified file paths and context lines to sd for targeted replacement.
View modified files at specific line numbers identified in the diff.
Search within files that appear in the diff for additional context.

compatible_upstream.description: Review the diff of files modified by find_replace before committing.
Generate a diff for a file previously viewed.
Diff files discovered via search to review recent changes.

vanquish.note: Diff content carries high-signal semantic information about code changes. File paths and line content are directly embeddable.

performance.note: Token pressure scales with number of changed files and lines. Use max_files and max_lines_per_file aggressively. stat_only mode reduces pressure to O(files).
